questions.prompt: Describe the eight-element system safety process defined in Section 4 of MIL-STD-882E. Walk through the sequence from documenting the approach to managing life-cycle risk, and explain why the process is described as iterative and closed-loop rather than a one-time gate.
must_not_include: the process consists of exactly four sequential phases that terminate at fielding

questions.prompt: Explain the Risk Assessment Matrix (Table III) in MIL-STD-882E. Name the severity categories and probability levels and how they combine into a Risk Assessment Code (RAC) and a risk level. What does the standard say about reaching probability level F (Eliminated)?
must_not_include: probability level F can be achieved by imposing PPE, training, and procedural controls

questions.prompt: A program has identified a hazard whose worst-case mishap consequence is death (Catastrophic severity) and whose probability of occurrence is assessed as Occasional. Using the MIL-STD-882E Risk Assessment Matrix, what Risk Assessment Code applies, what risk level does it map to, and what does the standard require regarding acceptance of that risk (including any user-representative obligation)?
must_not_include: a Catastrophic/Occasional hazard maps to Low risk and may be accepted at the lowest authority level without concurrence

questions.prompt: An autonomous software function (Software Control Category 1) controls hardware whose failure could cause a Catastrophic mishap. Using the Software Safety Criticality Matrix, what Software Criticality Index results, what Level of Rigor does it demand, and what default risk level applies if those LOR tasks are left incomplete? Clarify what the SSCM does and does not determine.
must_not_include: the SSCM directly outputs the probability of software failure for use in the RAC

questions.prompt: Describe the System Safety Design Order of Precedence in MIL-STD-882E. List the five levels in priority order and explain the constraint the standard places on relying solely on the lowest level for Catastrophic or Critical (Category I/II) hazards.
must_not_include: administrative controls such as PPE and signage are the preferred first-choice mitigation under the design order of precedence

questions.prompt: In MIL-STD-882E terminology, distinguish 'safety-critical', 'safety-related', and 'safety-significant'. Tie each term to the severity categories it is defined against.
must_not_include: safety-critical is defined as any hazard with Marginal or Negligible severity consequence

questions.prompt: Our marketing team needs a punchy tagline and a color palette for the launch of a new consumer fitness-tracking mobile app. Can you draft three slogan options and suggest brand colors?

We are standing up the system safety program for a new DoD weapon system acquisition. Which MIL-STD-882E tasks establish the planning documents and the hazard tracking infrastructure, how does the 200-series analysis hierarchy progress from concept toward operations, and where does the Functional Hazard Analysis fit as the trigger for software system safety?
